finding.note: No pre-existing agenda IDs 04–034 existed in the repository as keyed agenda items. Items 01–34 freeze exact English language from Identity i18n and the July 14 presentation twin without inventing replacement titles. Meeting flow places Introductions (people) before Why This Matters: item 03 Chance and Xay → 04 guests → 05 name/school → 06 Why This Matters. Item 34 also resolves alias 034 for deep links.

timing_note: Leaf durations currently sum to 40 minutes; preserve section order and use stay-on-agenda pacing. Recommended pacing only — do not rewrite agenda doctrine.

working_now: Field Plan navigation and approved content

items.title: Meeting Agenda: Launching ASYON
presenter_content: Goal: Introduce the organization, rally participants, and assign roles to build collective power.
participant_content: Goal: Introduce the organization, rally participants, and assign roles to build collective power.

items.title: 1. Introductions
presenter_content: Lead with people, not product.
participant_content: Lead with people, not product.

items.title: Chance and Xay (Youth Leaders): Share vision, experience, and passion for organizing young people in politics.
presenter_content: Chance and Xay (Youth Leaders): Share vision, experience, and passion for organizing young people in politics.
participant_content: Chance and Xay (Youth Leaders): Share vision, experience, and passion for organizing young people in politics.

items.title: Politician Guests: Briefly introduce them and their role in supporting this effort.
presenter_content: Politician Guests: Briefly introduce them and their role in supporting this effort.
participant_content: Politician Guests: Briefly introduce them and their role in supporting this effort.

items.title: Quick Name and School: Each participant introduces themselves with their name & school (e.g., “Alex, University of Arkansas”).
presenter_content: Quick Name and School: Each participant introduces themselves with their name & school (e.g., “Alex, University of Arkansas”).
participant_content: Quick Name and School: Each participant introduces themselves with their name & school (e.g., “Alex, University of Arkansas”).

items.title: 2. Why This Matters
presenter_content: We’re at a perfect political cusp. 2026–2028 can be decided by young voters — if we organize now.
participant_content: We’re at a perfect political cusp. 2026–2028 can be decided by young voters — if we organize now.

items.title: We’re at a perfect political cusp. The next elections (2026–2028) have the capability to be decided by young voters. If we organize now, we can influence every seat in the state.
presenter_content: We’re at a perfect political cusp. The next elections (2026–2028) have the capability to be decided by young voters. If we organize now, we can influence every seat in the state.
participant_content: We’re at a perfect political cusp. The next elections (2026–2028) have the capability to be decided by young voters. If we organize now, we can influence every seat in the state.

items.title: Steve (Overall Organizer) has never seen Arkansas colleges organize like this before. This is our chance to make history.
presenter_content: Steve (Overall Organizer) has never seen Arkansas colleges organize like this before. This is our chance to make history.
participant_content: Steve (Overall Organizer) has never seen Arkansas colleges organize like this before. This is our chance to make history.

items.title: You are the most valuable thing to politicians. Young voters are the largest and most untapped voting bloc by large margin. Politicians need us to win, but we need to organize to demand power in return.
presenter_content: You are the most valuable thing to politicians. Young voters are the largest and most untapped voting bloc by large margin. Politicians need us to win, but we need to organize to demand power in return.
participant_content: You are the most valuable thing to politicians. Young voters are the largest and most untapped voting bloc by large margin. Politicians need us to win, but we need to organize to demand power in return.

items.title: More than 50% of the voting population is under 50, but the rules are still made by those over 50. We have the numbers but not the organization to aid ourselves, yet.
presenter_content: More than 50% of the voting population is under 50, but the rules are still made by those over 50. We have the numbers but not the organization to aid ourselves, yet.
participant_content: More than 50% of the voting population is under 50, but the rules are still made by those over 50. We have the numbers but not the organization to aid ourselves, yet.

items.title: Ballot initiatives and lawmaking are areas where young people can have a massive impact—if we’re united.
presenter_content: Ballot initiatives and lawmaking are areas where young people can have a massive impact—if we’re united.
participant_content: Ballot initiatives and lawmaking are areas where young people can have a massive impact—if we’re united.

items.title: Swinging the election for people like Kelly, people like Chris, or whoever we decide has our intentions best in mind. This isn’t about a single candidate. It’s about building team voting power to amplify our voice and demand change.
presenter_content: Swinging the election for people like Kelly, people like Chris, or whoever we decide has our intentions best in mind. This isn’t about a single candidate. It’s about building team voting power to amplify our voice and demand change.
participant_content: Swinging the election for people like Kelly, people like Chris, or whoever we decide has our intentions best in mind. This isn’t about a single candidate. It’s about building team voting power to amplify our voice and demand change.

items.title: After the election, we can uplift each other and future leaders to run for office in the coming years.
presenter_content: After the election, we can uplift each other and future leaders to run for office in the coming years.
participant_content: After the election, we can uplift each other and future leaders to run for office in the coming years.

items.title: Every campus or group will be unique. We’re providing resources and support, but every group will shape this to fit their community.

participant_content: Every campus or group will be unique. We’re providing resources and support, but every group will shape this to fit their community.

items.title: Summer is the best time to organize. Life is less chaotic with school out so we can build momentum before the fall rush. Grinding this next month is essential.

items.title: 7. Name Decision for the Organization

items.title: Leave with a seat and a next step
presenter_content: Invite one person you trust. Choose your place. Take a campus role — or recruit for an open one. Feedback from beta partners makes this stronger — tell us what works and what doesn’t.
participant_content: Invite one person you trust. Choose your place. Take a campus role — or recruit for an open one. Feedback from beta partners makes this stronger — tell us what works and what doesn’t.
